ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stock_exchange_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "ticker" TEXT /* Stock ticker symbol (e.g., COST) */,
  "exchange_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the stock exchange */,
  "curr_date" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "curr_time" TEXT /* Time of the data point */,
  "p_ask_price" REAL /* Current ask price of the stock */,
  "p_bid_price" REAL /* Current bid price of the stock */,
  "p_value" REAL /* Current value of the stock */,
  "p_volume" INTEGER /* Current trading volume of the stock */,
  "p_implied_volatility" REAL /* Current implied volatility of the stock */,
  "p_ask_size" INTEGER /* Current ask size of the stock */,
  "p_bid_size" INTEGER /* Current bid size of the stock */,
  "data_version" INTEGER /* Version of the data point */,
  "data_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data point (e.g., API, manual input) */,
  "data_reliability" REAL /* Reliability score of the data point */,
  "data_status" TEXT /* Status of the data point (e.g., active, archived, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stock_exchange_data_exchange_id FOREIGN KEY ("exchange_id") REFERENCES stock_exchanges ("exchange_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stock_exchange_data_ticker FOREIGN KEY ("ticker") REFERENCES stock_tickers ("ticker_symbol")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_exchanges" (
  "exchange_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock exchange */,
  "exchange_name" TEXT /* Name of the stock exchange */,
  "exchange_api" TEXT /* API endpoint for accessing data from the stock exchange */,
  "exchange_timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the stock exchange */,
  "exchange_open_time" TEXT /* Opening time of the stock exchange */,
  "exchange_close_time" TEXT /* Closing time of the stock exchange */,
  "exchange_status" TEXT /* Current status of the stock exchange (e.g., open, closed, holiday) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exchange_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_tickers" (
  "ticker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock ticker */,
  "ticker_symbol" TEXT /* Stock ticker symbol (e.g., COST) */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company associated with the stock ticker */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector of the company (e.g., Technology, Healthcare) */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the company (e.g., Software, Pharmaceuticals) */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the company is based */,
  "market_cap" REAL /* Market capitalization of the company */,
  "ipo_date" TEXT /* Initial Public Offering (IPO) date of the stock */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the ticker information was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., trader, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions the user has (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* When the user account was created */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent used for access (e.g., browser, device) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "access_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES stock_exchange_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
